qa.overall_assessment: BA's analysis for item F (hooks/tests/ underscore-prefix cleanliness) is accurate and actionable. All 5 dimensions verified. The spec correctly identifies all 7 rename targets, 3 files needing mktemp+trap, the 1 source-level stale comment, and no active callers. Minor evidence-quality metadata inconsistencies (evidence_type label, off-by-one line ranges) do not affect implementation correctness. The stale comment count discrepancy (BA says 1, Codex OOS-F says 2) is explained by the 2 auto-generated INDEX.md occurrences, which BA correctly deferred to doc-sync auto-regeneration. BA spec is fit for dev dispatch.

findings.finding: evidence_type field in context JSON is labeled 'inferred' while component_chain entries explicitly say 'verified_via: Read'. This is an internal metadata inconsistency — BA DID read the files directly but the top-level evidence_type label does not reflect that.
findings.evidence: context-20260522-080646-F.json line 85: 'evidence_type': 'inferred'; lines 136-145 component_chain: 'evidence': 'Read tool confirmed...', 'verified_via': 'Read'
findings.proposed_fix: OBSERVATION_ONLY: Change evidence_type to 'observed' or 'mixed' for accuracy. Non-blocking — the underlying data is correct.

findings.finding: BA's component_chain line ranges are slightly off-by-one: _ac1_verify.sh claimed '1-20' (actual file: 19 lines); _ac5_verify.sh claimed '1-67' (actual file: 66 lines). Critical /tmp path line numbers are correct: _ac1_verify.sh:3, _ac5_verify.sh:7.
findings.evidence: wc -l confirmed: _ac1_verify.sh = 19 lines, _ac5_verify.sh = 66 lines; _final_sweep.sh = 167 lines (matches BA claim)
findings.proposed_fix: OBSERVATION_ONLY: Correct line ranges to 1-19 and 1-66. Non-blocking — critical /tmp path locations are accurate.

findings.finding: BA correctly identified all /tmp write-side line numbers: _ac1_verify.sh:3 (/tmp/push.sh.exec), _ac5_verify.sh:7 (/tmp/step7.md), _final_sweep.sh:37,60,89 (/tmp/push.sh.exec, /tmp/push-analyst-phase7.py, /tmp/step7.md). All verified by direct file read.
findings.evidence: Direct read of all 7 files confirmed these exact line numbers

findings.finding: BA correctly identified which 4 files have NO /tmp paths (_ac3, _ac6, _ac9, _ac10), verified by grep scan.

BA correctly identified the stale comment at _final_sweep.sh:2: '# Final sweep — invoke each AC's verifier and print PASS/FAIL summary.' — the file embeds all logic inline, never invoking separate _acN_verify.sh scripts.
findings.evidence: Direct read of _final_sweep.sh confirmed line 2 text and that the file contains inline logic sections (# AC7, # AC2, # AC1 V1, etc.) not invocations of external verifiers

findings.finding: BA confirmed no active callers of old filenames in settings.json, agents/, commands/, hooks/, scripts/, policies/. Only INDEX.md files (auto-generated, excluded) and historical docs/dev/ artifacts (read-only, excluded) reference old names.
findings.evidence: grep -rn '_ac[0-9]*_verify|_final_sweep' across settings.json, agents/, commands/, scripts/, policies/ returned no results; hooks/ (excluding hooks/tests/ and hooks/INDEX.md) also returned no results

findings.finding: BA's scope coverage matches the user requirement: rename (R1), chmod (R2), mktemp+trap (R3), stale comment (R4). Must Have / Won't Have classifications are correct.
findings.evidence: User requirement (OOS-F Codex summary): 'test_*.sh 命名 + mktemp + trap + 755 perm + 2 stale comment' — all 4 categories addressed in BA spec

findings.finding: BA's scope_expansion.all_occurrences list includes hooks/tests/INDEX.md and hooks/INDEX.md but does not mention root INDEX.md (which also contains old names per Codex scan). All three are auto-generated and non-callers, so this incompleteness is non-blocking.
findings.evidence: Codex adversarial scan noted: 'root INDEX.md also contains old names at line ~11999'. BA's list is incomplete but consequence-free since all three are auto-generated.
findings.proposed_fix: OBSERVATION_ONLY: Add root INDEX.md to out_of_path_observed_but_not_touched list for completeness.

findings.finding: BA ran (or inferred from) git ls-files -s to confirm 100644 mode for all 7 target files and 100755 for test_push_sentinel_abort.sh. This was directly verified by QA.
findings.evidence: git ls-files -s hooks/tests/ confirmed: all 7 _ files at 100644, test_push_sentinel_abort.sh at 100755

findings.finding: BA verified /tmp paths across all 7 files and correctly identified exactly 3 as needing mktemp+trap. The specific scratch variables in _final_sweep.sh (3 distinct /tmp paths including one used inside an inline Python snippet at line 63) are all called out.

BA spec says 'fix one stale comment' (singular) but the original Codex OOS-F summary says '2 stale comment' (plural). After reading all 7 source files, QA finds only ONE source-level stale comment (_final_sweep.sh:2). The second occurrence is in auto-generated INDEX.md files (hooks/tests/INDEX.md:16 and hooks/INDEX.md:44), which mirror the same stale text. BA's treatment is correct — these auto-regenerate via doc-sync — but the count claim in the BA spec understates total occurrences.
findings.evidence: grep across all 7 _ac*.sh and _final_sweep.sh files: only _final_sweep.sh line 2 contains a stale comment in source code. hooks/tests/INDEX.md:16 and hooks/INDEX.md:44 also contain the stale text but are marked auto-generated by the doc-sync hook (posttool-doc-sync.py confirmed in settings.json).
findings.proposed_fix: OBSERVATION_ONLY: BA could clarify: 'fix 1 source-level stale comment in _final_sweep.sh:2 (2 additional occurrences in auto-generated INDEX.md files will self-correct via doc-sync after rename)'. The implementation approach is correct as-is.

findings.finding: BA verified _final_sweep.sh line 56 already uses 'bash hooks/tests/test_push_sentinel_abort.sh' (the correct new name). No self-reference fix needed. Directly confirmed by QA.
findings.evidence: sed -n '56p' _final_sweep.sh output: 'bash hooks/tests/test_push_sentinel_abort.sh >/dev/null 2>&1 && results+=("AC1 V5 PASS") || results+=("AC1 V5 FAIL")'

findings.finding: All 7 target files confirmed to exist in hooks/tests/ with 644 permissions. File sizes consistent with BA's line count claims (within 1 line for _ac1 and _ac5; exact for _final_sweep).
findings.evidence: ls -la hooks/tests/ output: _ac1_verify.sh (838 bytes, -rw-r--r--), _ac3_verify.sh (2219 bytes), _ac5_verify.sh (3181 bytes), _ac6_verify.sh (1722 bytes), _ac9_verify.sh (1202 bytes), _ac10_verify.sh (2138 bytes), _final_sweep.sh (8888 bytes)

findings.finding: BA's component_chain only lists 3 of the 7 files (the logic-bearing /tmp files). However, development_approach.files_to_modify lists all 7, and the rename mapping table is complete and accurate for all 7.
findings.evidence: Rename mapping in ticket verified: 7 rows, all correct old→new name pairs. development_approach.files_to_modify lists all 7 files explicitly.

findings.finding: BA's 7-file rename mapping is complete and correct. All mappings from _acN_verify.sh / _final_sweep.sh to test_acN_verify.sh / test_final_sweep.sh verified against actual filenames on disk.
findings.evidence: ls hooks/tests/ output shows exactly these 7 files with the old names; rename map in ticket covers all 7

findings.finding: AC3 verification recipe ('grep /tmp/ hooks/tests/test_ac1_verify.sh hooks/tests/test_ac5_verify.sh hooks/tests/test_final_sweep.sh' — expect no match) is literal and unambiguous. AC4 recipe ('grep -n invoke each hooks/tests/test_final_sweep.sh' — expect no match) is literal and correct. No spec-text vs execution drift detected.
findings.evidence: AC1-AC5 in ticket match their stated GIVEN/WHEN/THEN verifiers exactly; no substitution or paraphrase of AC text in verification recipes

findings.finding: The rename map is complete (7 entries covering all 7 source files) and internally consistent with the file list in scope, development_approach, and success_criteria.
findings.evidence: Cross-checked rename map against: scope.included (7 files listed), development_approach.files_to_modify (7 files), context.scope_boundary.in_user_path (7 files), success_criteria[0] ('No file with _ac or _final_sweep prefix')

codex_consult.feedback_summary: Codex confirmed PASS verdict with no blocking objections. All 5 codex findings classified OBSERVATION_ONLY: (1) '2 stale comments' vs BA '1 stale comment' — only 1 source-level stale comment found, other occurrences are auto-generated; (2) evidence_type metadata inconsistency (inferred vs verified_via Read) — non-blocking; (3) line ranges off by 1 for _ac1 and _ac5 — non-blocking, critical paths correct; (4) component_chain only covers 3 of 7 files — mitigated by development_approach and ACs covering all 7; (5) root INDEX.md old-name occurrence not listed in BA's scope — auto-generated, non-caller, non-blocking.
codex_consult.feedback_incorporated: Codex's finding #5 (root INDEX.md not listed in BA scope) added to scope_alignment minor findings. All other findings were already captured in QA's own analysis. PASS verdict confirmed.

all_findings.issue: evidence_type field is 'inferred' while component_chain entries explicitly say 'verified_via: Read' — internal metadata inconsistency
all_findings.recommendation: Change evidence_type to 'observed' or 'mixed' for accuracy

all_findings.issue: _ac1_verify.sh line range claimed '1-20' (actual: 19 lines); _ac5_verify.sh claimed '1-67' (actual: 66 lines)

BA spec says 'fix one stale comment' (singular) but Codex OOS-F summary says '2 stale comment'. Only 1 source-level stale comment exists (_final_sweep.sh:2); the second and third occurrences are in auto-generated INDEX.md files. BA's implementation approach is correct but the count is understated.
all_findings.recommendation: Clarify: 'fix 1 source-level stale comment (_final_sweep.sh:2); 2 additional occurrences in auto-generated INDEX.md files will self-correct via doc-sync'

all_findings.issue: Root-level INDEX.md also contains old names per Codex adversarial scan. Not listed in BA's all_occurrences or out_of_path_observed_but_not_touched. Non-blocking as it is auto-generated.
all_findings.recommendation: Add root INDEX.md to out_of_path_observed_but_not_touched for completeness
